questions.question: Florida’s new congressional map approved by the Republican-controlled Legislature could give Republicans up to how many more House seats?
questions.explanation: The map was described as one that could net Republicans up to four additional House seats in the November midterms.

questions.question: What did the Supreme Court do to Louisiana’s second majority-Black congressional district?
options: It ordered the state to add a third majority-Black district
It struck the district down as an unconstitutional gerrymander
It postponed the ruling until after the midterms
It approved the map under Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act
questions.explanation: The court struck down Louisiana’s second majority-Black district and in the process narrowed the Voting Rights Act’s main tool against racial vote dilution.

questions.question: Which agencies were left unfunded in the Department of Homeland Security deal Trump signed?
options: The Coast Guard and Secret Service
questions.explanation: The bill restored funding for TSA, FEMA, the Coast Guard, and the Secret Service, but left ICE and Border Patrol out while Republicans pursued a separate immigration enforcement bill.

questions.question: What did Trump say “would never have happened” if it had already been built?
options: The White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting scare
The Supreme Court ruling on Louisiana’s map
questions.explanation: Trump said the shooting scare at the Washington Hilton would never have happened with the “Militarily Top Secret Ballroom” under construction at the White House.

questions.question: What did the National Trust for Historic Preservation refuse to do regarding Trump’s White House ballroom plan?
options: Drop its lawsuit challenging the project
Release donor records tied to the lawsuit
Testify before Congress about preservation rules
questions.explanation: The group refused to abandon its lawsuit contesting the proposed $400 million White House ballroom despite pressure from the Justice Department.

questions.question: What was arranged in the Instagram photo that led to James Comey’s indictment?
questions.explanation: Federal prosecutors indicted Comey over a 2025 Instagram photo showing seashells arranged to read “86 47.”

questions.question: Who was charged with attempting to assassinate Trump after the White House Correspondents’ Dinner incident?
questions.explanation: The Justice Department charged Cole Tomas Allen in connection with the attempted attack, saying he had planned it for weeks and brought multiple weapons.

questions.question: Who did Trump name after withdrawing Dr. Casey Means as his surgeon general nominee?
questions.explanation: Trump pulled Casey Means and selected Dr. Nicole Saphier, a radiologist and longtime Fox News contributor, as his new pick for surgeon general.

questions.question: Which government body said it would investigate the Justice Department’s handling of the Jeffrey Epstein files?
questions.explanation: The Government Accountability Office said it would review how the Justice Department handled, redacted, and released the Epstein records.

questions.question: Why did Sen. Thom Tillis say he would stop blocking Kevin Warsh’s nomination to lead the Federal Reserve?
options: The Justice Department dropped its criminal probe of Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell
Warsh agreed to withdraw support for interest rate cuts before the election
Trump promised to nominate Tillis for Treasury secretary
The Senate Banking Committee voted unanimously to fast-track the nomination
questions.explanation: Tillis said he would stop blocking Warsh after the Justice Department dropped its criminal probe of Jerome Powell and assured him the review would not be used against Fed independence.
